title: Fantastic Russian Beef Pirozhki
description: A festive Russian dish featuring juicy beef and onions wrapped in shortcrust pastry, perfect for any meal or snack. This recipe uses ready rolled shortcrust pastry for convenience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the shortcrust pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and cook the onion until softened.
Add the ground beef to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until it is no longer pink.
Stir in the salt and black pepper to season the beef mixture.
Beat the egg in a small bowl and set aside.
Cut out circles of pastry using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture onto one half of each pastry circle.
Brush the edges of the pastry with the beaten egg and fold the other half of the pastry circle over the filling to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the pirozhki and use a fork to crimp the edges.
Place the pirozhki on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with the remaining egg.
Bake the pirozhki in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
